ID=g16291.t1;Parent=g16291;em_target=48698.ENSPFOP00000013034;em_score=677.0;em_evalue=2.19e-249;em_tcov=100.0;em_OGs=COG0003@1|root,KOG2825@2759|Eukaryota,38BC4@33154|Opisthokonta,3BBDN@33208|Metazoa,3CSBR@33213|Bilateria,484XK@7711|Chordata,4909D@7742|Vertebrata,4A07X@7898|Actinopterygii;em_COG_cat=P;em_desc=ATPase required for the post-translational delivery of tail-anchored (TA) proteins to the endoplasmic reticulum. Recognizes and selectively binds the transmembrane domain of TA proteins in the cytosol. This complex then targets to the endoplasmic reticulum by membrane-bound receptors, where the tail- anchored protein is released for insertion. This process is regulated by ATP binding and hydrolysis. ATP binding drives the homodimer towards the closed dimer state, facilitating recognition of newly synthesized TA membrane proteins. ATP hydrolysis is required for insertion. Subsequently, the homodimer reverts towards the open dimer state, lowering its affinity for the membrane-bound receptor, and returning it to the cytosol to initiate a new round of ID=g17281.t1;Parent=g17281;em_target=48698.ENSPFOP00000009854;em_score=635.0;em_evalue=9.44e-234;em_tcov=100.0;em_OGs=KOG3071@1|root,KOG3071@2759|Eukaryota,39X7S@33154|Opisthokonta,3BMJJ@33208|Metazoa,3D5YQ@33213|Bilateria,48C47@7711|Chordata,48VSV@7742|Vertebrata,49Y00@7898|Actinopterygii;em_COG_cat=I;em_desc=Catalyzes the first and rate-limiting reaction of the four that constitute the long-chain fatty acids elongation cycle. This endoplasmic reticulum-bound enzymatic process, allows the addition of 2 carbons to the chain of long- and very long-chain fatty acids VLCFAs per cycle. Condensing enzyme that exhibits activity toward saturated C18 to C26 acyl-CoA substrates, with the highest activity towards C22 0 acyl-CoA.
